profile.primarySource: NHMRC / NZ MoH Nutrient Reference Values for Australia and New Zealand (2006; sodium and fluoride revised 2017)
profile.crossReference: Food and Nutrition Board, IOM/NAS Dietary Reference Intakes tables (Elements and Vitamins), males 19-30
notes: targetConfidence: 'confirmed' = checked against an Australian source. 'crosschecked' = verified in the US DRI tables and Australia uses the same number. 'unconfirmed' = Australia sets a DIFFERENT number from the US, or no NRV exists; the value here is unverified.
Australia diverges from the US on: vitamin C, vitamin E, vitamin K, pantothenic acid, copper, manganese, phosphorus, selenium and zinc. Never substitute a US figure for these.
Under 19 uses the 14-18 band: calcium 1300 mg, iron 11 mg, phosphorus 1250 mg, chromium 35 ug, several vitamins lower.
NHMRC has draft updated NRVs in consultation, final publication expected during 2026.

alertRules.notes: A day qualifies for a nutrient only if coverage >= minCoveragePct and entry confidence >= minConfidence. Non-qualifying days are 'unknown' - they neither extend nor break a run. Alert fires when qualifyingDaysRequired qualifying days inside the trailing windowDays breach the threshold in that nutrient's direction. Nutrients whose targetConfidence is not in allowedTargetConfidence are displayed but never alert.

energy.label: Energy
energy.source: NRV EER: 10.8-13.8 MJ/day for a 76 kg male 19-30 depending on activity
energy.note: PLACEHOLDER - set from your own weight and training load.

protein.label: Protein
protein.source: NHMRC/NZ MoH NRV, males 19-30 (RDI 64 g, EAR 52 g)
protein.note: Deficiency floor, not a hypertrophy target. Aim ~1.6-2.2 g/kg and override.

fibre.label: Fibre
fibre.source: NRV AI men 19+; Suggested Dietary Target is 38 g

cholesterol.label: Cholesterol
cholesterol.source: NHMRC reviewed cholesterol and set no NRV

water.label: Water (total)
water.source: NRV AI men 19-30, total incl. food (~2.6 L as fluids)

caffeine.label: Caffeine
caffeine.source: No Australian NRV; 400 mg/day common adult guidance

omega3LC.label: Omega-3 (EPA+DHA+DPA)
omega3LC.source: NRV AI men; Suggested Dietary Target 610 mg

calcium.label: Calcium
calcium.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

phosphorus.label: Phosphorus
phosphorus.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 700 mg)

magnesium.label: Magnesium
magnesium.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

iron.label: Iron
iron.source: NHMRC/NZ MoH NRV, males 19-30 (RDI 8 mg, EAR 6 mg); US agrees

zinc.label: Zinc
zinc.source: NHMRC/NZ MoH NRV, males 19-30 (RDI 14 mg, EAR 12 mg). US RDA is 11 mg - do not use it.

iodine.label: Iodine
iodine.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

selenium.label: Selenium
selenium.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 55 ug)

copper.label: Copper
copper.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 0.9 mg)

manganese.label: Manganese
manganese.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 2.3 mg)

chromium.label: Chromium
chromium.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

molybdenum.label: Molybdenum
molybdenum.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

potassium.label: Potassium
potassium.source: NRV AI men 19+; not in the US table consulted

sodium.label: Sodium
sodium.source: Suggested Dietary Target (2017 revision); AI 460-920 mg

vitaminA.label: Vitamin A
vitaminA.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

thiamin.label: Thiamin (B1)
thiamin.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

riboflavin.label: Riboflavin (B2)
riboflavin.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

niacin.label: Niacin (B3)
niacin.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

vitaminB6.label: Vitamin B6
vitaminB6.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

vitaminB12.label: Vitamin B12
vitaminB12.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

folate.label: Folate
folate.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

pantothenic.label: Pantothenic acid (B5)
pantothenic.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 5 mg)

biotin.label: Biotin (B7)
biotin.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

choline.label: Choline
choline.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value

vitaminC.label: Vitamin C
vitaminC.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 90 mg) - the AU value is much lower

vitaminD.label: Vitamin D
vitaminD.source: US DRI table (IOM/NAS), males 19-30; Australia adopted the same value. Assumes sun exposure; most vitamin D is not dietary, so a low reading here is weak evidence.

vitaminE.label: Vitamin E
vitaminE.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 15 mg)

vitaminK.label: Vitamin K
vitaminK.source: Australia sets a different value from the US DRI - AU figure below is UNVERIFIED (US 120 ug)
